segments.text: The Lord told Moses,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Tell the Israelites: If a man or a woman makes a special promise to become a Nazirite,  to dedicate themselves to the Lord,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: they must not drink any wine or other alcoholic drink. They must not even drink vinegar made from wine or other alcoholic drink, or any grape juice or eat grapes or raisins.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The whole time they are dedicated to the Lord they must not eat anything from a grapevine, not even grape seeds or skins.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: They must not use a razor on their heads during the whole time of this promise of dedication. They must remain holy until their time of dedication to the Lord is finished. They must let their hair grow long.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: During this time of dedication to the Lord they must not go near a dead body.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Even if it's their father, mother, brother or sister who has died, they are not to make themselves unclean, because their uncut hair announces their dedication to God.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Through the whole time of their dedication they are to be holy to the Lord.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: However, if someone dies suddenly close to them, making them unclean, they must wait seven days, and on the seventh day when they are made clean again they are to shave their heads.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: On the eighth day they shall take two turtledoves or two young pigeons to the priest at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The priest will offer one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering to make them right, because they became guilty by being near the dead body. On that day they must re-dedicate themselves and let their hair grow again.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: They must re-dedicate themselves to the Lord for the full time they originally promised and bring a one-year-old male lamb as a guilt offering. The previous days don't count towards the time of dedication because they became unclean.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: These are the rules to be observed when the Nazirite's time of dedication is finished. They are to be taken to the entrance to the Tent of Meeting.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: There they are to present an offering to the Lord of a one-year-old male lamb without defects as a burnt offering, a one-year-old female lamb without defects as a sin offering, and a ram without defects as a peace offering.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: In addition they are to bring a basket of bread without yeast made from the best flour mixed with olive oil and wafers without yeast coated with olive oil as well as their grain offerings and drink offerings.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The priest will present all these before the Lord as well as sacrificing the sin offering and the burnt offering.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He will also sacrifice a ram as a peace offering to the Lord, together with the basket of bread made without yeast. In addition the priest will present the grain offering and drink offering.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Then the Nazirites are to shave their heads at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting. They shall take the hair from their heads that were dedicated, and place it on the fire under the peace offering.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Once the Nazirites have shaved, the priest will take the boiled shoulder from the ram, one bread without yeast from the basket, and one wafer without yeast, and place them in their hands.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The priest will wave them as a wave offering before the Lord. These items are holy and belong to the priest, as well as the breast of the wave offering and the thigh that was offered. Once this is finished, Nazirites may drink wine.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: These are the rules to be observed when a Nazirite promises to give offerings to the Lord regarding their dedication. They can also bring additional offerings if they can afford it. Every Nazirite must fulfill whatever promises they have made when they dedicated themselves.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The Lord told Moses,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Tell Aaron and his sons: This is how you are to bless the Israelites. This is what they are to say:
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: ‘May the Lord bless you and take care of you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: May the Lord smile on you and be gracious to you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: May the Lord watch over you and give you peace.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Whenever the priests bless the Israelites in my name, I will bless them.
segments.speaker: The LORD
